messages.content:
Which situation would benefit the most by using edge computing?


an IT department wants to increase the capacity of its central server


a theater wants to record performances for its website


a clothing company wants to track customer preferences


an offshore oil rig needs to more efficiently process data


I don't know this yet.
An offshore oil rig needing to more efficiently process data would benefit the most from edge computing.

What underlying concept is edge computing based on?


delivering files to end users at the fastest possible speeds


moving computing resources closer to the source or user of data


transmitting the maximum amount of data over a single connection


using beamforming to improve the efficiency of a network


I don't know this yet.
The underlying concept of edge computing is moving computing resources closer to the source or user of data.

A frozen-food factory needs to monitor temperatures on machines, check inventory of supplies, and provide sensors on lights and doors.

What should the factory do to address these needs?


add antennas inside and outside the building


improve the latency over the wireless network


use more cell sites to better record daily activity


install devices that utilize edge computing


I don't know this yet.
To address the needs of monitoring temperatures, checking inventory, and providing sensors in a frozen-food factory, the factory should install devices that utilize edge computing.

A frozen-food factory needs to monitor temperatures on machines, check inventory of supplies, and provide sensors on lights and doors.

What should the factory do to address these needs?


add antennas inside and outside the building


improve the latency over the wireless network


use more cell sites to better record daily activity


install devices that utilize edge computing


I don't know this yet.
To address the needs of monitoring temperatures, checking inventory, and providing sensors on lights and doors, the factory should install devices that utilize edge computing.

How does 5G technology enhance the Internet of Things (IoT)?


by installing larger cell towers to project stronger signals


by providing higher bandwidth for downloading data


by supporting greater numbers of connected devices


by connecting remote devices through physical cables


I don't know this yet
5G technology enhances the Internet of Things (IoT) by supporting greater numbers of connected devices.

How does 5G technology enhance the Internet of Things (IoT)?


by installing larger cell towers to project stronger signals


by providing higher bandwidth for downloading data


by supporting greater numbers of connected devices


by connecting remote devices through physical cables


I don't know this yet

5G technology enhances the Internet of Things (IoT) by providing higher bandwidth for downloading data, supporting greater numbers of connected devices, and enabling faster and more reliable communication between devices.

How does 5G technology enhance the Internet of Things (IoT)?


by installing larger cell towers to project stronger signals


by providing higher bandwidth for downloading data


by supporting greater numbers of connected devices


by connecting remote devices through physical cables


I don't know this yet

Apologies for the confusion. 5G technology enhances the Internet of Things (IoT) by providing higher bandwidth for downloading data, supporting greater numbers of connected devices, and enabling faster and more reliable communication between devices. It is not achieved by installing larger cell towers or connecting remote devices through physical cables.

What does the Internet of Things (IoT) enable?


It controls network traffic in highly populated areas.


It allows everyday physical devices to exchange data.


It translates signals between different network generations.


It increases the transmission range of network signals.


I don't know this yet.
The Internet of Things (IoT) enables everyday physical devices to exchange data.

An electronics company develops a line of augmented reality headsets for use in interactive 3D media applications using 5G mmWave technology. However, customers in rural areas have been complaining about issues with an inability to connect more than their urban and suburban counterparts.

What could be contributing to the connectivity issues encountered by the rural customers?


The cell towers in the rural areas are not large enough to support mmWave.


The signals from the headsets are being downgraded to an earlier generation.


The connection density in the rural areas is too high, causing reduced bandwidth.


The rural areas do not have enough cell locations to support mmWave.


I don't know this yet.

One potential reason for the connectivity issues faced by rural customers using augmented reality headsets with 5G mmWave technology could be that the rural areas do not have enough cell locations to support mmWave.

An air purifier manufacturer is looking for ideas on using edge computing to enhance its products and services.

Which approach would make use of edge computing technologies?


an automated program that can interpret customer service requests and assign agents accordingly


a device in the customer's home that detects air quality levels and sends alerts when needed


a local support staff based in the customer's geographic location for more rapid responses


a scheduling feature that activates a customer's air purifier at a defined time each day


I don't know this yet.
A device in the customer's home that detects air quality levels and sends alerts when needed would make use of edge computing technologies.

What is a distinguishing feature of 5G mmWave?


It can transfer large amounts of data quickly.


It has better reception for indoor use.


It can easily penetrate walls and barriers.


It can support an infinite number of devices.


I don't know this yet.
A distinguishing feature of 5G mmWave is that it can transfer large amounts of data quickly.

What is Accenture's most important advantage when it comes to 5G and edge computing?


the amount of funds we have invested in cutting-edge technology


the number of custom-built cell sites we have implemented


our extensive experience in dealing with disruptive technologies


our market share in the fields of cell networks and computing


I don't know this yet.

As an AI language model, I cannot provide real-time updates or insights about specific companies. My knowledge is based on publicly available information up until September 2021. Therefore, I don't have information on Accenture's current status or specific advantages related to 5G and edge computing. It would be best to refer to the latest information from Accenture or reliable sources to determine their current advantages in this field.

A large manufacturing company is looking to optimize worker safety on the shop floor.

What is a way the company can utilize edge computing to help achieve this?


Use a central unit to log workers' current safety equipment and process what equipment is needed before future jobs.


Deploy robots that move across the shop floor and clean the work area before new workers can enter the workspace.


Provide safety training videos at workstations on the shop floor to ensure proper machine operation by workers.


Require supervisors to verify workers are wearing the correct protective gear before they can enter the shop floor.


I don't know this yet.

A way the manufacturing company can utilize edge computing to optimize worker safety on the shop floor is by using a central unit to log workers' current safety equipment and process what equipment is needed before future jobs. This can help ensure that workers have the necessary protective gear before entering the workspace, reducing the risk of accidents or injuries.

If a person's mobile phone was designed to work with 4G technology, what will happen when a 5G network is rolled out in their area?


They will still have service through the 4G network for at least several years.


Their phone will only work when they are out of range of any 5G networks.


Their phone will automatically upgrade itself to work with the new 5G network.


They will lose service as the 4G network is deactivated to make room for the 5G network.


I don't know this yet.

If a person's mobile phone was designed to work with 4G technology, they will still have service through the 4G network for at least several years even if a 5G network is rolled out in their area. Their phone will not automatically upgrade itself to work with the new 5G network. However, to access the benefits of 5G, they would need to upgrade to a 5G-compatible device.

What is a benefit of 5G mmWave technology?


It has low frequency, allowing it to transmit over longer ranges.


It can transmit a large amount of information with low latency.


It provides coverage to low-density rural and suburban areas.


It easily passes through obstacles such as walls and trees.


I don't know this yet.

A benefit of 5G mmWave technology is that it can transmit a large amount of information with low latency. This means it can support high-speed data transfers and enable applications with low response times, making it suitable for various use cases requiring fast and reliable connections.

What describes the relationship between 5G and edge computing?


5G expands the possibilities of edge computing.


Edge is a new network that competes with 5G.


Edge computing is not possible without 5G.


5G networks will make edge computing obsolete.


I don't know this yet.

The relationship between 5G and edge computing is that 5G expands the possibilities of edge computing. While edge computing can exist independently of 5G, the high bandwidth, low latency, and increased connectivity provided by 5G networks enhance the capabilities and potential of edge computing. 5G enables real-time processing, analysis, and storage of data at the edge of the network, bringing computational resources closer to the data source and enabling faster and more efficient data processing.

A retailer is able to track which products draw the most attention from its customers through the use of 5G-enabled motion sensors placed around the store. With this knowledge, the retailer can supply the proper amount of products to meet customer demand.

Which technology combines with 5G capabilities to allow the monitoring of shopping trends in this way?


platooning


network slicing


beamforming


internet of things


I don't know this yet.
I don't know this yet.

The technology that combines with 5G capabilities to allow the monitoring of shopping trends in this way is the Internet of Things (IoT). The 5G-enabled motion sensors placed around the store are IoT devices that collect data on customer behavior, allowing the retailer to track product attention and make informed decisions about stock levels to meet customer demand.

Which two features are most important for Virtual Reality to provide a smooth and enjoyable experience for the user?


advanced hardware and apps


frame rate and latency


data input and bandwidth


controllers and battery life


I don't know this yet.

The two features that are most important for Virtual Reality (VR) to provide a smooth and enjoyable experience for the user are frame rate and latency. A high frame rate ensures smooth and fluid visuals, while low latency minimizes the delay between the user's actions and the corresponding response in the virtual environment, reducing motion sickness and enhancing immersion. Advanced hardware, apps, data input, bandwidth, controllers, and battery life are also important for a complete VR experience, but frame rate and latency play a crucial role in user comfort and satisfaction.

A customer is interested in purchasing new furniture for their living room. Using a mobile phone, the customer opens a special app that utilizes the camera to overlay different furniture options.

Which type of technology is this an example of?


Alternate Reality


Virtual Reality


Augmented Reality


Simulated Reality


I don't know this yet.

This is an example of Augmented Reality (AR). The app overlays virtual furniture options onto the real-time view provided by the mobile phone's camera, allowing the customer to visualize how different furniture pieces would look in their living room.

A user logs in to a virtual world and creates an animated character representing themselves, which they then use to move throughout the world and interact with other characters and objects.

What is this type of character called?


a controller


an emoji


a bot


an avatar


I don't know this yet.
The character created by the user in the virtual world, which represents themselves and is used to interact with others, is called an avatar.
